# Implementation Plan: Lark Bitable CLI for AI Bug Triage

**Branch**: `001-lark-bitable-cli` | **Date**: 2026-05-07 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-lark-bitable-cli/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit-plan` and incorporates the user
constraint to implement the feature in TypeScript while using mature existing
CLI and Lark packages where available.

## Summary

Build a TypeScript CLI that lets a developer interactively log in to Lark,
configure a Lark Base/Bitable URL, inspect records, filter/search bug data, run
readiness validation, guided bug triage, and produce an evidence-backed first
research report for AI-assisted debugging. The tool will also ship AI-facing
bootstrap skill/install guidance so agents can discover the CLI, verify
installation, and follow the supported workflow without guessing.

Use existing packages instead of custom framework code: oclif for multi-command
CLI structure, the official Lark Node SDK for Lark OpenAPI access,
`@inquirer/prompts` for login/config/triage prompts, `conf` and `zod` for
persisted configuration and validation, and Vitest for TypeScript tests.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x on Node.js 22 LTS-compatible runtime  
**Primary Dependencies**: oclif (`oclif` CLI + `@oclif/core`),
`@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk`, `@inquirer/prompts`, `conf`, `zod`, `vitest`  
**Storage**: Local per-user CLI configuration for active Bitable source, field
mappings, Lark app settings, and last triage selection stored outside the repo at
`~/.lark-bitable/config.json`; Lark auth state stored outside the repo at
`~/.lark-bitable/auth.json`; generated research reports written to
repository-local files  
**Testing**: Vitest for unit and integration-style CLI tests; mocked Lark client
responses for deterministic validation  
**Target Platform**: Developer workstations and AI-agent execution environments that can run Node.js CLI tools  
**Project Type**: TypeScript CLI application with installable AI bootstrap skill assets  
**Performance Goals**: Users can complete Lark login in under 3 minutes when
authorization is available; users can configure a source in under 2 minutes;
`valid` identifies blocking setup gaps with remediation; list/filter/search at
least 50 accessible records without opening Lark manually; guided triage
produces sorted choices in a single workflow  
**Constraints**: Read-focused first version; no destructive Lark writes; every
factual report claim must cite bug record, repository, command, user input, or
runtime evidence; raw Lark tokens must never appear in logs or reports;
bootstrap and Lark API commands must fail closed when install/config/auth
prerequisites are missing  
**Scale/Scope**: One CLI package, one bootstrap skill package, commands for
login/logout/valid/configure/list/get/filter/search/triage/research/help/doctor,
and one feature's planning artifacts

## Constitution Check

_GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design._

- **AI consumer contract**: PASS. The plan defines explicit AI-facing commands,
  structured outputs, login/auth preconditions, `valid` remediation output,
  bootstrap installation guidance, self-check behavior, and report sections for
  facts, assumptions, analysis, risks, and next actions.
- **Evidence sources**: PASS. Planning evidence comes from the feature spec,
  current repository files, command output from setup and package version checks,
  Lark official documentation observed in Chrome DevTools, and package/source
  research documented in [research.md](./research.md).
- **Unsupported-claim prevention**: PASS. Example Lark table rows remain marked
  as unverified because DevTools did not expose row values. Package decisions
  record current lookup dates and alternatives rather than claiming permanence.
- **Reproducible validation**: PASS. Quickstart and contracts define login,
  `valid`, self-check, command examples, mocked validation flows, and report
  evidence requirements.
- **Report traceability**: PASS. Research reports must separate facts,
  assumptions, analysis, risks, and next actions, and must cite selected bug
  record and repository evidence.

Post-design re-check: PASS. `data-model.md`, `contracts/cli-contract.md`, and
`quickstart.md` preserve evidence metadata, fail-closed login/validation/
bootstrap behavior, token redaction, and non-destructive Lark access.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-lark-bitable-cli/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   └── cli-contract.md
└── tasks.md
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
package.json
tsconfig.json
vitest.config.ts
src/
├── cli/
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── configure.ts
│   │   ├── lark.ts
│   │   ├── valid.ts
│   │   ├── list.ts
│   │   ├── get.ts
│   │   ├── filter.ts
│   │   ├── search.ts
│   │   ├── triage.ts
│   │   ├── research.ts
│   │   └── doctor.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── lark/
│   ├── client.ts
│   ├── auth.ts
│   ├── url-parser.ts
│   └── record-mapper.ts
├── config/
│   ├── store.ts
│   ├── auth-store.ts
│   ├── readiness.ts
│   └── schema.ts
├── triage/
│   ├── candidate-sort.ts
│   └── selection-state.ts
├── reporting/
│   ├── evidence.ts
│   └── research-report.ts
└── bootstrap/
    ├── skill/
    │   └── SKILL.md
    └── installer.ts

tests/
├── unit/
├── integration/
└── fixtures/
```

**Structure Decision**: Use a single TypeScript CLI package at repository root.
Group code by responsibility rather than by command only: CLI command adapters,
Lark login/access, persisted config, auth storage, triage logic, reporting, and
bootstrap skill assets. Keep tests under `tests/` with mocked Lark responses so
validation does not require live table access.

## Complexity Tracking

No Constitution Check violations require justification. The plan uses existing
frameworks and explicit evidence gates instead of custom CLI framework or
unsupported report generation.
